entity_relationships.business_meaning: A department contains many employees (one-to-many). Each employee belongs to exactly one department.

entity_relationships.related_entities: Department, Role, Shift, Schedule, Attendance, LeaveRequest, TrainingRecord, Equipment, MaintenanceRequest, CleaningTask, TurnaroundOperation
entity_relationships.business_meaning: An employee belongs to one department (many-to-one), holds multiple roles via EmployeeRole junction (many-to-many), works shifts through schedules (one-to-many Schedule), generates attendance records (one-to-many), submits leave requests (one-to-many), completes training records (one-to-many), can be assigned equipment (many-to-many via EquipmentAssignment), may be assigned cleaning tasks (one-to-many), and participates in turnaround operations (many-to-many via task assignments).
A role can be assigned to many employees through the EmployeeRole junction (many-to-many). Each employee-role assignment links one employee to one role.
A shift defines a work period and can be assigned to many employees via Schedule (one-to-many). Each schedule entry links one employee to one shift for a specific date.
A schedule assigns an employee to a shift on a specific date (many-to-one to Employee, many-to-one to Shift). It represents the planned roster.
An attendance record captures an employee's actual clock-in/out for a date (many-to-one to Employee). Each employee can have multiple attendance records.
A leave request is submitted by an employee (many-to-one). An employee can have multiple leave requests over time.
A training record documents an employee's completion of a training course (many-to-one). An employee can have multiple training records.
An equipment type categorizes many equipment items (one-to-many). Each piece of equipment belongs to exactly one type.
Equipment belongs to one type (many-to-one), can be assigned to multiple employees via EquipmentAssignment (many-to-many), and generates maintenance requests (one-to-many).
A maintenance request is raised for a specific equipment item (many-to-one). Each equipment can have multiple maintenance requests; maintenance history tracks the resolution.

entity_relationships.related_entities: Gate, Stand, TurnaroundOperation, Baggage, GateAssignment, StandAssignment
entity_relationships.business_meaning: A flight is assigned to one gate and one stand via GateAssignment and StandAssignment (many-to-one each), undergoes a turnaround operation (one-to-one), and carries multiple baggage items (one-to-many).
A gate can be assigned to many flights over time via GateAssignment (one-to-many). Each gate assignment links one gate to one flight for a period.
A stand can be assigned to many flights via StandAssignment (one-to-many). Each stand assignment links one stand to one flight.

A turnaround operation is tied to one flight (one-to-one), involves multiple tasks (boarding, refueling, catering, cleaning) each assigned to employees (many-to-many via task tables), and handles baggage loading/unloading (one-to-many to BaggageLoading/Unloading).

A baggage item belongs to one flight (many-to-one), is scanned at checkpoints (one-to-many BaggageScan), may be loaded/unloaded during turnaround (one-to-many BaggageLoading/Unloading), and can be reported lost (one-to-one LostBaggage).

business_processes.tables: departmentsss, employee, attendance, role, employee_role, shift, schedule, leave_request, training_record
flight, gate, stand, gate_assignment, stand_assignment, turnaround_operation, turnaround_checklist, boarding_task, refueling_task, catering_task, cleaning_task

use_cases.name: Daily Flight Turnaround Management
use_cases.description: Coordinate all ground handling tasks (boarding, refueling, catering, cleaning, baggage) for a flight's turnaround, tracking task completion and responsible employees.
use_cases.involved_tables: flight, turnaround_operation, boarding_task, refueling_task, catering_task, cleaning_task, baggage_loading, baggage_unloading, employee

use_cases.name: Employee Shift and Attendance Tracking
use_cases.description: Manage employee rosters by assigning shifts, recording actual attendance, and reconciling scheduled vs actual hours for payroll.

use_cases.name: Equipment Maintenance Lifecycle
use_cases.description: Track equipment assignments, schedule preventive maintenance, log repair history, and ensure equipment availability for operations.
use_cases.involved_tables: equipment, equipment_type, equipment_assignment, maintenance_request, maintenance_history, employee

use_cases.name: Baggage Tracking and Lost Baggage Resolution
use_cases.description: Monitor baggage from check-in through loading, transfer, unloading, and delivery; identify and resolve lost baggage cases.
use_cases.involved_tables: baggage, baggage_scan, baggage_loading, baggage_unloading, lost_baggage, flight

use_cases.name: Gate and Stand Allocation Planning
use_cases.description: Assign gates and stands to flights based on schedule, aircraft size, and operational constraints; manage conflicts and changes.

sample_queries.question: List all employees who were scheduled for a shift but did not clock in on a specific date.
sample_queries.sql: SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, s.shift_id, s.shift_name, sch.schedule_date FROM employee e JOIN schedule sch ON e.employee_id = sch.employee_id JOIN shift s ON sch.shift_id = s.shift_id LEFT JOIN attendance a ON e.employee_id = a.employee_id AND a.attendance_date = sch.schedule_date WHERE a.attendance_id IS NULL AND sch.schedule_date = '2024-01-15';

sample_queries.question: Show the total number of baggage items loaded per flight for the last week.
sample_queries.sql: SELECT f.flight_number, f.scheduled_departure, COUNT(bl.baggage_id) AS bags_loaded FROM flight f JOIN baggage_loading bl ON f.flight_id = bl.flight_id WHERE f.scheduled_departure >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '7 days' GROUP BY f.flight_id, f.flight_number, f.scheduled_departure ORDER BY f.scheduled_departure;

sample_queries.question: Find equipment that has had more than 3 maintenance requests in the past 6 months.
sample_queries.sql: SELECT eq.equipment_id, eq.equipment_name, et.type_name, COUNT(mr.request_id) AS request_count FROM equipment eq JOIN equipment_type et ON eq.equipment_type_id = et.equipment_type_id JOIN maintenance_request mr ON eq.equipment_id = mr.equipment_id WHERE mr.request_date >= CURRENT_DATE - mr.request_date <= INTERVAL '6 months' GROUP BY eq.equipment_id, eq.equipment_name, et.type_name HAVING COUNT(mr.request_id) > 3 ORDER BY request_count DESC;

sample_queries.question: Calculate average turnaround time per flight type (domestic vs international) for completed turnarounds.
sample_queries.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN f.destination_airport LIKE 'INT%' THEN 'International' ELSE 'Domestic' END AS flight_type, AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (to_actual_end - to_actual_start))/60) AS avg_turnaround_minutes FROM flight f JOIN turnaround_operation to ON f.flight_id = to.flight_id WHERE to.to_actual_start IS NOT NULL AND to.to_actual_end IS NOT NULL GROUP BY flight_type;

sample_queries.question: Retrieve all cleaning tasks assigned to a specific employee during a given turnaround operation.
sample_queries.sql: SELECT ct.task_id, ct.task_description, ct.status, ct.start_time, ct.end_time FROM cleaning_task ct JOIN turnaround_operation to ON ct.turnaround_id = to.turnaround_id WHERE ct.assigned_employee_id = 123 AND to.flight_id = 456 ORDER BY ct.start_time;

validation_report.message: Foreign key employee.department_id references table 'department', which does not exist in the schema.
